reasoning: Strong performance: flags multiple clear instances from the author’s own prose with appropriate labels and no attribution violations. Minor misses of additional biased framing (e.g., laudatory and rhetorical framing of Trump as a bipartisan unifier and the closing celebration line).

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text is the author’s own prose from an opinion piece; no quoted-source language was treated as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged item contains evaluative, emotive, or persuasive language clearly in the author’s voice; none are neutral or misattributed.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed some notable author-level bias such as the opening ‘bipartisan unifier?’/‘arguably most important reforms’, colloquial ‘picking up the tab,’ and the concluding ‘worth celebrating.’
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels match the instances well (e.g., ‘travesty’ as opinion-as-fact, ‘finally’ as spin, emotive ‘descent into sickness and even death’ as sensationalism).
